title: Is Big Tech spending more just to stand still in the AI race?
excerpt: Big Tech's AI spending is soaring, but higher capex may reflect inflation as much as expansion this earnings season.
content_html:
Big Tech earnings season and the capex spiral

By Alistair Barr

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Author of the Tech Memo newsletter

Big Tech earnings season starts next week with Google, but the most important number won't be profit or revenue. It will be how much these companies say they'll spend on AI data centers.

Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta have already laid out plans to spend more than $700 billion this year. Investors know the companies are racing to add computing power. The harder question is how much extra capacity those dollars actually buy.

The answer is getting worse. Memory chip prices have surged, while power equipment, construction materials, skilled workers, and electricity connections are all harder to secure.

Morgan Stanley now estimates the cost of building one gigawatt of AI capacity has risen about 20% for several leading systems. One common Nvidia-based setup, for example, has gone from roughly $29 billion to $35 billion per gigawatt; a newer version has climbed from $41 billion to $49 billion.

That creates a nasty loop. Big Tech orders more AI data center gear. Shortages worsen. Prices rise. The companies then raise spending forecasts partly to cover those higher prices, which creates still more demand and even higher prices.

Brad Gastwirth, head of research at Circular Technology, estimates about 20% to 30% of the next increase in AI capex will reflect inflation, while 70% to 80% will still represent real expansion. In other words, higher spending doesn't always mean the AI buildout is accelerating at the same rate.

"Investors need to be careful when interpreting higher capex. There is absolutely an inflation component," Gastwirth told me recently.

That distinction matters to investors. Earlier research found that soaring memory prices could explain about 45% of the growth in capex by the big cloud companies this year.

Cantor Fitzgerald analysts expect little change to 2026 capex plans this earnings season, but see 2027 estimates moving sharply higher, including $283 billion for Google, $271 billion for Amazon, and $200 billion for Meta.

So, will anyone blink and stop increasing capex? Probably not yet. No company wants to look cautious while AI rivals are racing ahead. But listen closely to what accompanies any spending increase this time.

"When the companies discuss capex, I'd pay close attention to whether they're also talking about power capacity, GPU deployments, memory purchases, networking, and new data center campuses," Gastwirth said. "If capex rises alongside those metrics, it points to genuine expansion rather than simply higher costs."

Without those details, a larger capex number may simply mean Big Tech is paying more to stand still.

Sign up for BI's Tech Memo newsletter here. Reach out to me via email at abarr@businessinsider.com.

Read next

Alistair Barr

Alistair won a Gerald Loeb Award in 2007 for coverage of short selling and was a finalist in 2013 for scoops on the Facebook IPO. blocks.html_en: Big Tech earnings season starts next week with Google, but the most important number won't be profit or revenue. It will be how much these companies say they'll spend on AI data centers.

sentences.en: Big Tech earnings season starts next week with Google, but the most important number won't be profit or revenue.
sentences.zh: 谷歌的大型科技财报季将于下周开始，但最重要的数字不是利润或收入。

sentences.en: It will be how much these companies say they'll spend on AI data centers.
sentences.zh: 这将是这些公司表示将在人工智能数据中心上花费多少钱。

blocks.html_en: Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta have already laid out plans to spend more than $700 billion this year. Investors know the companies are racing to add computing power. The harder question is how much extra capacity those dollars actually buy.

sentences.en: Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta have already laid out plans to spend more than $700 billion this year.
sentences.zh: 谷歌、亚马逊、微软和 Meta 已经制定了今年支出超过 7000 亿美元的计划。

sentences.en: Investors know the companies are racing to add computing power.
sentences.zh: 投资者知道这些公司正在竞相增加计算能力。

sentences.en: The harder question is how much extra capacity those dollars actually buy.
sentences.zh: 更难的问题是这些美元实际购买了多少额外容量。

blocks.html_en: The answer is getting worse. Memory chip prices have surged, while power equipment, construction materials, skilled workers, and electricity connections are all harder to secure.

sentences.en: The answer is getting worse.
sentences.zh: 答案越来越糟糕。

sentences.en: Memory chip prices have surged, while power equipment, construction materials, skilled workers, and electricity connections are all harder to secure.
sentences.zh: 存储芯片价格飙升，而电力设备、建筑材料、技术工人和电力连接都更难保障。

blocks.html_en: Morgan Stanley now estimates the cost of building one gigawatt of AI capacity has risen about 20% for several leading systems. One common Nvidia-based setup, for example, has gone from roughly $29 billion to $35 billion per gigawatt; a newer version has climbed from $41 billion to $49 billion.

sentences.en: Morgan Stanley now estimates the cost of building one gigawatt of AI capacity has risen about 20% for several leading systems.
sentences.zh: 摩根士丹利现在估计，对于几个领先的系统来说，建设 1 吉瓦人工智能容量的成本已上升约 20%。

sentences.en: One common Nvidia-based setup, for example, has gone from roughly $29 billion to $35 billion per gigawatt; a newer version has climbed from $41 billion to $49 billion.
sentences.zh: 例如，一种基于 Nvidia 的常见设置每千兆瓦的成本已从大约 290 亿美元增至 350 亿美元；新版本已从 410 亿美元攀升至 490 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: That creates a nasty loop. Big Tech orders more AI data center gear. Shortages worsen. Prices rise. The companies then raise spending forecasts partly to cover those higher prices, which creates still more demand and even higher prices.

sentences.en: That creates a nasty loop.
sentences.zh: 这就造成了一个令人讨厌的循环。

sentences.en: Big Tech orders more AI data center gear.
sentences.zh: 大型科技公司订购了更多人工智能数据中心设备。

sentences.en: Shortages worsen.
sentences.zh: 短缺情况进一步恶化。

sentences.en: Prices rise.
sentences.zh: 价格上涨。

sentences.en: The companies then raise spending forecasts partly to cover those higher prices, which creates still more demand and even higher prices.
sentences.zh: 随后，这些公司提高了支出预测，部分原因是为了应对价格上涨，从而创造更多需求，甚至推高价格。

blocks.html_en: Brad Gastwirth, head of research at Circular Technology, estimates about 20% to 30% of the next increase in AI capex will reflect inflation, while 70% to 80% will still represent real expansion. In other words, higher spending doesn't always mean the AI buildout is accelerating at the same rate.

sentences.en: Brad Gastwirth, head of research at Circular Technology, estimates about 20% to 30% of the next increase in AI capex will reflect inflation, while 70% to 80% will still represent real expansion.
sentences.zh: Circular Technology 研究主管 Brad Gastwirth 估计，人工智能资本支出下次增长的约 20% 至 30% 将反映通货膨胀，而 70% 至 80% 仍将代表实际扩张。

sentences.en: In other words, higher spending doesn't always mean the AI buildout is accelerating at the same rate.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，更高的支出并不总是意味着人工智能的建设正在以相同的速度加速。

blocks.html_en: "Investors need to be careful when interpreting higher capex. There is absolutely an inflation component," Gastwirth told me recently.

sentences.en: "Investors need to be careful when interpreting higher capex.
sentences.zh: “投资者在解读更高的资本支出时需要小心。

sentences.en: There is absolutely an inflation component," Gastwirth told me recently.
sentences.zh: 绝对有通货膨胀的成分，”加斯特沃斯最近告诉我。

blocks.html_en: That distinction matters to investors. Earlier research found that soaring memory prices could explain about 45% of the growth in capex by the big cloud companies this year.

sentences.en: That distinction matters to investors.
sentences.zh: 这种区别对投资者来说很重要。

sentences.en: Earlier research found that soaring memory prices could explain about 45% of the growth in capex by the big cloud companies this year.
sentences.zh: 此前的研究发现，今年大型云公司资本支出增长的约 45% 是由内存价格飙升造成的。

blocks.html_en: Cantor Fitzgerald analysts expect little change to 2026 capex plans this earnings season, but see 2027 estimates moving sharply higher, including $283 billion for Google, $271 billion for Amazon, and $200 billion for Meta.

sentences.en: Cantor Fitzgerald analysts expect little change to 2026 capex plans this earnings season, but see 2027 estimates moving sharply higher, including $283 billion for Google, $271 billion for Amazon, and $200 billion for Meta.
sentences.zh: Cantor Fitzgerald 分析师预计，本财报季 2026 年资本支出计划不会有太大变化，但预计 2027 年资本支出预计将大幅上升，其中谷歌为 2830 亿美元，亚马逊为 2710 亿美元，Meta 为 2000 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: So, will anyone blink and stop increasing capex? Probably not yet. No company wants to look cautious while AI rivals are racing ahead. But listen closely to what accompanies any spending increase this time.

sentences.en: So, will anyone blink and stop increasing capex?
sentences.zh: 那么，有人会眨眼并停止增加资本支出吗？

sentences.en: Probably not yet.
sentences.zh: 可能还没有。

sentences.en: No company wants to look cautious while AI rivals are racing ahead.
sentences.zh: 当人工智能竞争对手一路领先时，没有一家公司愿意表现得谨慎。

sentences.en: But listen closely to what accompanies any spending increase this time.
sentences.zh: 但请仔细聆听这次支出增加会带来什么。

blocks.html_en: "When the companies discuss capex, I'd pay close attention to whether they're also talking about power capacity, GPU deployments, memory purchases, networking, and new data center campuses," Gastwirth said. "If capex rises alongside those metrics, it points to genuine expansion rather than simply higher costs."

sentences.en: "When the companies discuss capex, I'd pay close attention to whether they're also talking about power capacity, GPU deployments, memory purchases, networking, and new data center campuses," Gastwirth said.
sentences.zh: Gastwirth 表示：“当公司讨论资本支出时，我会密切关注他们是否也在谈论电力容量、GPU 部署、内存采购、网络和新数据中心园区。”

sentences.en: "If capex rises alongside those metrics, it points to genuine expansion rather than simply higher costs."
sentences.zh: “如果资本支出与这些指标一起上升，则表明是真正的扩张，而不仅仅是成本上升。”

blocks.html_en: Without those details, a larger capex number may simply mean Big Tech is paying more to stand still.

sentences.en: Without those details, a larger capex number may simply mean Big Tech is paying more to stand still.
sentences.zh: 如果没有这些细节，更大的资本支出数字可能仅仅意味着大型科技公司要付出更多代价才能维持现状。

大型科技公司的财报季和资本支出螺旋式上升

作者：Alistair Barr 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 《技术备忘录》时事通讯的作者

谷歌的大型科技财报季将于下周开始，但最重要的数字不是利润或收入。 这将是这些公司表示将在人工智能数据中心上花费多少钱。

谷歌、亚马逊、微软和 Meta 已经制定了今年支出超过 7000 亿美元的计划。 投资者知道这些公司正在竞相增加计算能力。 更难的问题是这些美元实际购买了多少额外容量。

答案越来越糟糕。 存储芯片价格飙升，而电力设备、建筑材料、技术工人和电力连接都更难保障。

摩根士丹利现在估计，对于几个领先的系统来说，建设 1 吉瓦人工智能容量的成本已上升约 20%。 例如，一种基于 Nvidia 的常见设置每千兆瓦的成本已从大约 290 亿美元增至 350 亿美元；新版本已从 410 亿美元攀升至 490 亿美元。

这就造成了一个令人讨厌的循环。 大型科技公司订购了更多人工智能数据中心设备。 短缺情况进一步恶化。 价格上涨。 随后，这些公司提高了支出预测，部分原因是为了应对价格上涨，从而创造更多需求，甚至推高价格。

Circular Technology 研究主管 Brad Gastwirth 估计，人工智能资本支出下次增长的约 20% 至 30% 将反映通货膨胀，而 70% 至 80% 仍将代表实际扩张。 换句话说，更高的支出并不总是意味着人工智能的建设正在以相同的速度加速。

“投资者在解读更高的资本支出时需要小心。 绝对有通货膨胀的成分，”加斯特沃斯最近告诉我。

这种区别对投资者来说很重要。 此前的研究发现，今年大型云公司资本支出增长的约 45% 是由内存价格飙升造成的。

Cantor Fitzgerald 分析师预计，本财报季 2026 年资本支出计划不会有太大变化，但预计 2027 年资本支出预计将大幅上升，其中谷歌为 2830 亿美元，亚马逊为 2710 亿美元，Meta 为 2000 亿美元。

那么，有人会眨眼并停止增加资本支出吗？ 可能还没有。 当人工智能竞争对手一路领先时，没有一家公司愿意表现得谨慎。 但请仔细聆听这次支出增加会带来什么。

Gastwirth 表示：“当公司讨论资本支出时，我会密切关注他们是否也在谈论电力容量、GPU 部署、内存采购、网络和新数据中心园区。” “如果资本支出与这些指标一起上升，则表明是真正的扩张，而不仅仅是成本上升。”

如果没有这些细节，更大的资本支出数字可能仅仅意味着大型科技公司要付出更多代价才能维持现状。
